Who I Am

Builder.
Researcher.
Student.

GitHub LinkedIn Email

I’m a first-year B.Tech CSE student at SRMIST Kattankulathur, building at the edges of post-quantum cryptography and assistive technology — two fields I believe will define the next decade.

I built FreeFace, a hands-free OS controller for users with ALS and SCI, and a CRYSTALS-Kyber hybrid encryption framework aligned with NIST FIPS 203. Both are production-grade and open-source.

Outside of code: F1 race strategy, cricket, quantum circuits, and obsessing over elegant systems.

Selected Work

Things I’ve built.

Production-grade projects at the intersection of cryptography, AI, and human-computer interaction.

01
FreeFace — Hands-Free OS Controller
Full assistive OS controller for users with ALS, SCI, and cerebral palsy. Gaze, blink, eyebrow, head nod, voice. C++ engine + Python ctypes + MediaPipe + per-user SVM calibration.
C++PythonMediaPipeSVM
↗ 02
Post-Quantum Secure Exchange Framework
Hybrid quantum-resistant key-exchange: CRYSTALS-Kyber (NIST FIPS 203) + AES-256-GCM via FastAPI. Architecture mirrors 2025 SSH/TLS upgrades by GitHub & Cloudflare.
PythonKyberFastAPIliboqs
↗ 03
Quantum Lab — Circuit Simulation Platform
Full-stack quantum simulator: 20+ gates, real-time statevector playback, Bloch sphere visualization, dual-circuit comparison, variational optimization up to 8 qubits.
Next.jsTypeScriptQiskitWebSocket
↗ 04
APEX AI — F1 Race Strategy Engine
Tire degradation prediction and optimal pit-stop timing using edge computing. XGBoost vs LSTM benchmarking across multi-race FastF1 datasets.
PythonXGBoostLSTMEdge
↗ 05
Chess-Based Encryption (CBED) — Patent Pending
Novel cipher using chess-piece move sequences (Shannon number: 10¹²&sup0;) as keys. Benchmarked vs AES-128. Presented to 200+ at inter-school science expo. Rem-Mark1 model.
PythonC++CryptographyPatent
↗
